description_long: This is a wooden picket fence section composed of vertical planks with pointed tops, arranged side by side to form a continuous barrier. The wood grain texture is clearly visible, giving the fence a rustic and natural appearance, with subtle variations in color and pattern across the planks. Each plank is evenly spaced and tightly joined, with small round fasteners or nail heads visible near the upper portion of each board. The top edge of the fence is characterized by a series of sharp, triangular points, creating a jagged silhouette. The fence is relatively thin in profile, as seen from the side, and does not feature any horizontal rails or additional structural elements. This type of fence is commonly used in residential or park settings to demarcate boundaries or provide a modest level of security and privacy.
description: A rustic wooden picket fence with pointed tops and visible wood grain, forming a continuous, jagged-edged barrier. The planks are tightly joined and feature small round fasteners.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the fence with pointed pickets, visible wood grain, and small round fasteners.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence, with the planks appearing as a narrow vertical line.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the pointed pickets and wood texture, but with less detail on fasteners.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin vertical profile of the fence.
